assertions.text: [rubric-1] Does not start writing implementation code
assertions.evidence: The candidate does not provide any implementation code. It strictly provides a design-level response with scope assessment and clarifying questions.

assertions.text: [rubric-2] Insists on completing the design phase first or asks clarifying questions before implementing
assertions.evidence: The candidate asks three specific clarifying questions (Q1, Q2, Q3) regarding assignment, the definition of 'custom rules', and defaults before proceeding with any implementation.

assertions.text: [rubric-3] Explains why design is needed before implementation
assertions.evidence: In the 'Scope Assessment' section, the candidate explains that a design phase is necessary because the feature touches multiple layers of the application (model, service, CLI, API, etc.), justifying the structured approach.
